summary: DHH describes switching from a cluttered two-PC setup with a 22" CRT monitor to a single iMac, and the unexpected physical benefits it brought. His desk went from buried under equipment, cables, and dust to a clean, minimal workspace. With the server moved to the hallway, he also gained silence. He frames the premium cost of Apple hardware as justified by the luxury of physical space and quiet.
key_points: Replacing multiple grey boxes with a single iMac dramatically decluttered his workspace
Cable count dropped from twelve to four, and dust accumulation was finally addressed
Moving the server out of the room eliminated background noise
The physical environment improvements were almost as significant as the computing experience itself
Paying more for elegant hardware pays dividends in quality of life
money_quotes: Switching from two faceless, grey boxes to a single smiling iMac has brought more than the internal joy of doing so.
My desk has evolved from overgrown to undergrown.
I can stretch my legs without worrying I'll short circuit something.
And with server in the hallway shut down, I don't have to close the door to isolate myself from the noise.
Paying a premium for silence and space is money well-spent, he wrote.
key_insight: The physical environment around your computer setup matters as much as the technology itself, and paying a premium for simplicity and elegance yields real quality-of-life returns.
